verdict.moderator_pairing_notes: The moderator correctly identified a consensus in Round 1 and terminated the debate. While the consensus was factually incorrect (all agents failed to identify Peru), the procedural decision to stop debating was correct given the agents' agreement.
verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge failed to identify the error in the agents' reasoning. The agents relied on 'soft evidence' (lush vegetation, mountains) which is common to many countries (Peru, Colombia, Ecuador, etc.). The judge did not apply the evidence hierarchy to challenge the agents' lack of 'hard constraints' (like specific text or unique regulatory details) and simply accepted the majority vote. The prediction (Colombia) was incorrect.
